content.full_text:
May 8, 1990

Kathy Donovan, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Kathy:

I am writing to confirm the telephone advice you gave me on May 8, 1990, regarding gifts to the State. You informed me that in each of the following instances, a gift has been made to the State, and there are no reporting obligations on the part of the donor or of the Assembly or its members in connection with the gift.

1. A member introduces an individual to the Assembly during floor session. Following the introduction, there is a reception for the individual. A corporation wants to pay for the reception. The corporation may make a gift to the Assembly Rules Committee to cover the cost of the reception. The Chief Administrative Officer of the Rules Committee writes to the corporation accepting the gift on behalf of the Assembly. Invitations to the reception (typically to all Members) are extended by the Rules Committee or by a Member of the Assembly (authorized by Rules), without mentioning the corporation. The corporation does not extend any invitations to the reception.

2. A person gives a gift of a permanent object, such as a statue, to the Assembly. The Rules Committee accepts the gift (by a letter from the Chief Administrative Officer). The Rules Committee controls the gift, which may be placed in a Member's office. Ownership of the gift remains in the Assembly; the Member cannot take the gift with him or her when leaving office.

Kathy Donovan, General Counsel  
Page Two  
May 8, 1990

3. A person gives a gift to the Assembly of objects which will be used by the Assembly or its members, such as books or city maps. The gift is to Rules Committee, and is accepted by a letter from the Chief Administrative Officer. Rules Committee controls the gifts, which may be given to individual members for their use. The objects may be used in the same way as anything else provided to the members by the Assembly. They may be given to visitors to legislative offices, for example, if that is the appropriate use.

The Rules Committee plans to follow the procedures in this letter regarding gifts to the Assembly.

Sincerely,

Barbara Milman  
Staff Counsel

BM:bml
